claim: The root `CLAUDE.md` shall contain a DOMAIN-MAP block listing every `domains/<name>/` with id, description, goals, director, path, atoms-count.
why: The framework operator must be able to discover all domains without reading each domain's graph. The DOMAIN-MAP block is generated by RenderDomainMapBlock (internal/generator/claudemd.go) wrapped in the DOMAIN-MAP sentinel block by the root CLAUDE.md renderer, so it cannot drift from the actual directory layout (R-deterministic-generation): the renderer walks the domains/ directory on disk, loads each domain's graph.json for the SETTLED atom count, and looks up presentation fields (id, description, goals, director) from the code-level knownDomainManifests map. atoms-count gives the operator a quick load-budget estimate (R-context-budget-rule). The on-disk manifest.json carries only self_hosting in the Go port, so the description/goals/director fields are sourced from knownDomainManifests (with em-dash fallback for unknown domains) rather than the manifest file; a future create_domain port would persist them on disk.
